tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan

payload.description: The MTA released a draft plan for the Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign – the first overhaul of Brooklyn bus service in decades. The proposals create a simpler bus network to encourage bus ridership, reduce travel time and strengthen interborough bus travel. The plan proposes 69 local routes and 19 express routes, for a total of 88 routes.

tool_args.label: Customer Service Centers in Subway Stations

payload.description: Comprehensive Customer Service Centers in the subway system providing direct customer support with OMNY use and Reduced-Fare support. Will open in six subway stations in early 2023, with nine additional stations expected later in the year.

tool_args.label: Station Agents Customer Service Improvements

payload.description: Historic agreement with TWU Local 100 to modernize the station agent role, bringing agents out of booths to perform core customer service functions at turnstiles, fare machines, and throughout stations. Training underway on enhanced customer service and OMNY equipment.

tool_args.label: All-Electric Bus Fleet Transition

payload.description: Commitment to transition to a fully all-electric fleet by 2040. 2020-2024 Capital Program includes funding for up to 500 electric buses and charging infrastructure. Public solicitation of bids for 470 battery-electric buses (380 standard and 90 articulated). Buses projected to be deployed at 11 depots, delivered in two tranches: first projected in 2025, rest in 2026.

tool_args.label: Fuel Management System Upgrade at Bus Depots

payload.status: requesting Board approval to award contract
payload.description: Contract to Fleetwatch for $6.35M to upgrade fuel management systems at bus depots. Fleetwatch currently deployed at Grand Avenue and Mother Clara Hale depots. Contract will enable installation at all remaining bus depots over two years. System expected to last approximately 20 years.

tool_args.label: Bus Lane Enforcement Camera Expansion

payload.description: Expanded Bus lane enforcement cameras on 300 additional buses and 9 new routes. Final three routes activated on December 12th include B25, B62, and B42. Extends onboard camera coverage to 50% of existing bus lanes serving 265K (pre-pandemic) average weekday customers across all five boroughs. Goal to expand with 600 additional cameras by end of 2023, covering up to 85% of existing bus lanes.
Bus lane enforcement camera activated December 12, 2022 as part of 9 new routes
Bus lane enforcement camera activated December 12, 2022 as part of 9 new routes
Bus lane enforcement camera activated December 12, 2022 as part of 9 new routes

tool_args.label: Customer Service Centers opening in early 2023

payload.description: Customer Service Centers will open in six subway stations in early 2023, with nine additional stations expected later in the year.

tool_args.label: Bus Lane Enforcement Cameras Activated on Final Three Routes

payload.description: Final three bus lane enforcement camera routes (B25, B62, B42) activated, extending onboard camera coverage to 50% of existing bus lanes.

tool_args.label: 7th TransitTalk at Utica Avenue A/C Station

payload.description: 7th TransitTalk event at Utica Avenue A/C station in Brooklyn, providing one-on-one engagement with customers at their neighborhood station.

tool_args.label: Reduced Fare OMNY Event at Bay Ridge Senior Center

payload.description: MTA Government and Community Relations and Systemwide Accessibility teams held event at Bay Ridge Center in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn to discuss OMNY rollout and Reduced Fare options, joined by NYS Senator Andrew Gounardes.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Public Workshops

payload.description: 18 public workshops to gather input on Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan from customers and Brooklyn residents.

tool_args.label: New Post-COVID Subway Ridership High

payload.description: New post-COVID high of 3.93 million subway riders on December 8th, likely due to shopping and tourist travel for holidays.

tool_args.label: Station Agents New Role Phased In

payload.description: Department of Subways will begin to phase in the new station agent role in early 2023, with training underway on enhanced customer service and OMNY equipment.

tool_args.label: Public Solicitation of Bids for 470 Battery-Electric Buses

payload.description: Public solicitation of bids for 470 battery-electric buses (380 standard and 90 articulated), projected to be deployed at 11 depots. First tranche projected in 2025, rest in 2026.

tool_args.label: November 2022 Subway Customer Satisfaction

payload.description: Overall subway customer satisfaction was 58% in November

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Customer Satisfaction

payload.description: Bus customer satisfaction was 68% in November, up from 65% in October

tool_args.label: November 2022 Paratransit Customer Satisfaction

payload.description: Access-A-Ride customer satisfaction declined to 65% in November

tool_args.label: November 2022 Subway Service Delivered

payload.description: Service Delivered was 94.5% in November, the highest since April 2021

tool_args.label: November 2022 Customer Journey Time Performance

payload.description: Customer Journey Time Performance reached 84.1%, the second highest since May 2021

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Service Delivered

payload.description: Bus Service Delivered remained above 95% since June and is up 1.5% compared to November last year

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Speed

payload.description: Bus speeds remained stable at 8.0 mph compared to last year, and up 1.3% compared to 2019

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Customer Journey Time Performance

payload.description: Bus customer journey time performance was 71.3% in November

tool_args.label: November 2022 Subway On-Time Performance

payload.description: On-Time Performance was 81.9%, an increase from the past two months, but down compared to last November

tool_args.label: November 2022 Average Subway Paid Weekday Ridership

payload.description: Average paid weekday subway ridership declined 1.9% from 3.55 million to 3.48 million

tool_args.label: November 2022 Average Bus Paid Weekday Ridership

payload.description: Average paid weekday bus ridership declined 2.4% from 1.45 million to 1.41 million

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bronx AM Weekday Speed Improvement

payload.description: AM weekday speeds were up 9% on the changed routes in the Bronx compared to 2019

tool_args.label: November 2022 Subway Ridership as % of Pre-COVID

payload.description: Ridership on school-open weekdays stood at 61.3% of pre-COVID levels on subway

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Ridership as % of Pre-COVID

payload.description: Ridership on school-open weekdays stood at 63.8% of pre-COVID levels on bus

tool_args.label: Post-COVID Subway Ridership High on December 8, 2022

payload.description: New post-COVID high of 3.93 million riders on December 8th

payload.raw_value_text: 95% of trips within the 30-minute pick-up window
payload.description: Primary carrier service completed 95% of trips within the 30-minute pick-up window, better than goal of 94%

tool_args.label: November 2022 Paratransit Broker On-Time Performance

payload.raw_value_text: 93% of trips within the 30-minute pick-up window
payload.description: Broker service completed 93% of trips within the 30-minute pick-up window, slightly under goal

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue Variance from Mid-Year Forecast

payload.description: Farebox revenue was favorable to the Mid-Year Forecast by $126.6 million due primarily to a higher average fare than projected

payload.description: Ridership on November weekends was 74% of comparable pre-COVID weekends on subway

tool_args.label: November 2022 Bus Weekend Ridership vs Pre-COVID

payload.description: Ridership on November weekends was 66% of comparable pre-COVID weekends on bus

payload.raw_value_text: increase of 6 percentage points over the past six months
payload.description: Subway customer experience ratings demonstrated an increase of 6 percentage points over the past six months

payload.raw_value_text: 72% improvement on primary carrier from October 2021
payload.description: Overall No-Show performance per 1,000 scheduled trips significantly improved by 72% on primary carrier from October 2021

payload.raw_value_text: expanded Bus lane enforcement cameras on 300 additional buses and 9 new routes

tool_args.label: Speed Unit Established October 2021

payload.claim_text: The Department of Subways will deliver a presentation on the Year-End results yielded from the Speed Unit established in October 2021. This group works with all departments at NYCT to increase train speeds, reduce delays, and improve operating efficiency.
payload.subject: Department of Subways Speed Unit

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Color-Coded Route Types

payload.claim_text: The Draft Plan proposes five color-coded route types: green for Local routes, purple for Rushroutes, red for Limited routes, blue for Crosstown (SBS) routes, and dark green for Express routes.
payload.subject: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan

tool_args.label: Subway Lines with Over 90% On-Time Performance

payload.claim_text: In November, the 7, G, L, and all three shuttles had over 90% on time performance.
payload.subject: subway line performance

warnings: claim.routes is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route arrays should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Population Growth Since 2010

payload.claim_text: The borough's population has grown 5.2% since 2010 and according to Census data, and about 55% of Brooklyn households do not own a vehicle.
payload.subject: Brooklyn demographics

tool_args.label: Station Agent Hiring Numbers

payload.claim_text: 288 new station agents have been hired since November 2021 and we anticipate hiring an additional 235 through the first half of 2023.
payload.subject: station agent hiring

tool_args.label: ADA Settlement Agreement for Accessibility by 2055

payload.claim_text: This year we announced a settlement agreement with disability and transit advocates that provides a clear path toward accessibility across the subway system by 2055 — putting a timeline to this commitment for the first time.
payload.subject: subway accessibility

tool_args.label: Bus Open Stroller Pilot Started October 2022

payload.claim_text: In October we started our Bus Open Stroller Pilot, allowing parents and caregivers to bring an open, unfolded stroller on seven select NYCT and MTA bus routes.
payload.subject: Bus Open Stroller Pilot

tool_args.label: NYCT Faster, Cleaner, Safer Plan

payload.claim_text: As part of our NYCT Faster, Cleaner, Safer Plan, this month the Department of Subways will deliver a presentation on the Year-End results yielded from the Speed Unit established in October 2021.
payload.subject: NYCT Faster Cleaner Safer Plan

tool_args.label: Zoning for Accessibility First Year Implementation

payload.claim_text: This was also the first year of implementation for Zoning for Accessibility, which is already yielding dividends with private developers committing to adding elevators at stations in Manhattan and Queens.
payload.subject: Zoning for Accessibility
